commit.message:
Address PTL migration TODO/FIXME follow-ups (#832)

It supports restoring legacy EMA (Exponential Moving Average) weights from older checkpoint formats, ensuring backward compatibility when resuming training. The main changes include updating checkpoint handling logic, enhancing the EMA callback to restore legacy states, and adding comprehensive tests for the new behavior.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add guarded clean-room TUM RGB-D sanity lane (#18)

* feat(rgbd): add clean-room TUM RGB-D sanity lane

Summary:
- add a public TUM RGB-D manifest, runner, and report generation path
  for upstream ORB-SLAM3 `rgbd_tum`
- add a clean-room orchestration command with progress artifact updates
  for fetch, bootstrap, build, and run phases
- document the new public dataset workspace area and operator commands

Rationale:
- HEL-61 needs a reproducible proof path that starts from a fresh clone,
  rebuilds upstream ORB-SLAM3, and leaves auditable runtime artifacts
- keeping the dataset lane public and pinned makes it possible to debug
  upstream failures without relying on private user recordings

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_rgbd_tum_baseline
  tests.test_operator_scripts tests.test_harness

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(rgbd): guard clean-room build retries

Summary:
- record ORB-SLAM3 build-attempt metadata and reject identical rebuilds
  unless the attempt is explicitly changed or overridden
- force the clean-room RGB-D runner to use the portable build flags and
  fail immediately when `rgbd_tum` or `libORB_SLAM3.so` is missing
- extend operator-script tests to pin the retry guard and output checks

Rationale:
- the prior clean-room pass stalled mid-build and left no trajectories,
  so the next attempt needs machine-readable attempt metadata and a
  concrete changed variable instead of another silent rerun
- treating missing build outputs as an immediate failure prevents the
  orchestration layer from overstating progress when upstream never
  produced a runnable binary

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_operator_scripts
  tests.test_rgbd_tum_baseline

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(rgbd): capture build failures and cap parallelism

---------

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(ci): avoid false release version mismatches

Summary:
- switch the release tag check to mix eval --no-compile so it reads the package version without compiling the project
- record the release workflow fix under Unreleased in the changelog

Rationale:
- a fresh GitHub runner let mix run emit compile output into package_version, which made the v0.4.0 tag check fail even though mix.exs matched the tag
- keeping the fix in changelog documents the release automation regression for the next cut

Validation:
- reproduced the failed shell comparison locally with a fresh MIX_BUILD_PATH and mix run
- verified mix eval --no-compile returns only 0.4.0 and makes the shell comparison pass
- confirmed GitHub Actions run 23313188711 failed in the Check tag matches package version step

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(runtime): tighten timeout handling and link tooling

Summary:
- complete late reliable one-sided redundant bounces inside the original
- frame budget instead of depending on an extra merge-window timer hop
- normalize domain transport-miss health to :timeout when realtime work
- expires in the bus queue and add regression coverage for that path
- update the maintained redundant replug watcher and docs to use the
- current Bus.info/1 link and topology surface

Rationale:
- cyclic runtime state should describe the transport miss honestly without
- oscillating between queue-expiry and frame-timeout labels
- maintained hardware tooling should match the current runtime surface so
- release-time diagnostics do not fail on stale field names or topologies

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/domain_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/01_transient_timeout_test.exs --trace
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(operator): harden timeline session continuity

Summary:
- normalize operator timeline timestamps so queued native notifications work with their real stored ISO string values
- scope recent intervention loading by session before limiting so session-specific timelines keep linked continuity items
- add endpoint and repository regressions for string notification timestamps, session-scoped intervention loading, and queued insight thread mapping

Rationale:
- the new operator timeline would 500 whenever pending native notifications existed because their created_at field is stored as a string
- session-filtering after a global intervention cap could silently drop relevant intervention-linked items from thread-specific timelines

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_guardian_feedback.py
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix grouped item selection, ungrouping, and nesting

Restore expected whiteboard group behavior across hit-testing, selection, and nesting.

This change removes the group-first hit-testing shortcut so items inside groups can be targeted directly for drag interactions, while selection promotion still resolves grouped children back to their parent group for click selection. The select tool now treats clicks on grouped children consistently with selected groups instead of force-selecting every child.

Ungroup now explicitly detaches children from the group before removing the container so grouped items return to the board as plain items instead of being left in broken nested state. Groups are also allowed to be nested again, with ancestor checks to prevent cyclic containment.

Add focused regression coverage for:
- hit-testing a child inside a group
- selecting a group by clicking a grouped child
- ungrouping 3 items back into plain board items
- nesting one group inside another

Verified with:
- bun test src/Items/Group/__tests__/Group.test.ts src/Items/Group/__tests__/GroupLocking.test.ts src/Items/Group/__tests__/GroupRegression.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix plugin manifest metadata

Summary:

- the recovery and redundant-bounce fix commit

- the repo changelog rules require release-note-worthy entries to point
- at the concrete change hash before handoff

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(recovery): heal held slaves and split redundant bounces

Summary:
- keep slave health polling active while slaves are held in PREOP
- or SAFEOP and align simulator docs/tests with PREOP-first
- reconnect healing
- keep a bounded merge window for complementary redundant logical
- bounce replies so split-ring raw topologies can recover full WKC
- suppress duplicate raw simulator exclusion warnings in test helper

Rationale:
- held slave states still need to surface disconnects and lower AL-state
- regressions instead of leaving stale runtime state behind
- split redundant bounce replies can each carry partial logical payload, so
- accepting the first processed bounce too early causes false recovery
- duplicate interface warnings added noise without improving test feedback

Validation:
- mix test --failed
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

style: format rust server and core modules

Apply formatting-only cleanup across Rust core/server files and related tests without behavior changes.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex (GPT-5) <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(platform): tighten operator continuity guidance

Summary:
- repair starter-pack actions for skill-only tool blockers and keep workflow approval rows usable from run-level approval data
- lower overall guardian confidence when memory retrieval degrades instead of leaving it grounded
- add backend and cockpit regressions for the review-reported paths

Rationale:
- operator guidance should only surface actionable repair and continuation paths
- degraded memory should be reflected consistently in the synthesized guardian state used downstream

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_capabilities_api.py tests/test_guardian_state.py
- cd frontend && npm test -- src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(slave): own reconnect healing and held-state monitoring

Summary:
- move reconnect ownership into the slave runtime using stored
  position probes, anonymous fixed-station reclaim, and local PREOP
  rebuilds
- remove master reconnect authorization and rediscovery fallback from
  the active reconnect path, and align docs and tests to that model
- keep PREOP and SAFEOP health polling active so disconnects and
  lower-state regressions enter recovery instead of stranding stale
  held states

Rationale:
- the common power-loss return case is local to one slave and is
  simpler to heal in one place than to orchestrate through the master
- intentionally held PREOP and SAFEOP sessions are still live runtime
  states, so they should keep fault visibility instead of going silent

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_recovery_bus_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/05_safeop_retreat_with_health_poll_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/03_disconnect_with_health_poll_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/43_permanent_slave_disconnect_recovery_stuck_test.exs

commit.message:
Refactor tracker integrations into state-sync skill repos (#66) closes #55 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | The orchestrator loaded tracker code from `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/{tracker,github,local}` and read runtime config from `[tracker]` / `kind`. | The orchestrator loads the shared interface from `state-sync/`, the GitHub/local implementations from `state-sync-github/` and `state-sync-local/`, and reads runtime config from `[state]` / `type`. | | Observable effect | GitHub merge automation helpers lived on the shared tracker boundary, and the repo had no shared round-trip contract test for implementations. | GitHub-only merge automation lives under `state-sync-github/merge-automation/`, the shared boundary is implementation-neutral, and `state-sync/test` now drives round-trip contract checks for GitHub and local implementations. | | Affected inputs | `tracker.kind`, `tracker.*` runtime config, local project `[projects..adapter]`, and legacy tracker module paths. | `state.type`, `state.*` runtime config, local project `[projects..state]`, and the new root-level state-sync folder layout. | ```mermaid flowchart LR Orchestrator --> Interface[state-sync/interface] Interface --> Github[state-sync-github/adapter] Interface --> Local[state-sync-local/adapter] Github --> Merge[state-sync-github/merge-automation] Github --> Contract[state-sync/test] Local --> Contract ``` ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: orchestrator dispatch, issue polling, local state transitions, GitHub issue syncing, startup scripts, dynamic tool gating, and validation commands must keep working after the rename/reorg. - **Explicitly unchanged**: the orchestrator runtime model, existing Linear support, and the underlying GitHub/local API behavior stay in place; this PR reorganizes boundaries and config naming without changing the overall orchestration workflow. - **Out of scope**: introducing a new remote implementation beyond GitHub/local, or changing the user-facing GitHub workflow process beyond the config and folder rename. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | shared state-sync contract regresses across implementations | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/state_sync_round_trip_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | | broad orchestrator/config/runtime regressions from the rename | `make -C elixir all` | N/A (local command) | | config compatibility paths break during migration | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/workspace_and_config_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/core_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: runtime config parsing or implementation selection could drift if a caller still expects `tracker.kind` semantics. - **Blast radius**: orchestrator startup, dynamic tool gating, GitHub/local state-sync modules, startup scripts, and tests/docs that consume runtime config. - **How to detect**: `make -C elixir all` fails; startup script tests fail; or runtime config parsing returns missing/unsupported state-type errors. - **How to rollback**: revert commit `5d44dcb` to restore the pre-reorg tracker layout and config surface. ### Review Focus 1. Verify the new `state-sync/`, `state-sync-github/`, and `state-sync-local/` boundaries keep GitHub-only logic out of the shared interface. 2. Verify the config migration/compatibility path from `tracker.kind` to `state.type`, including local project `[projects..state]` parsing. 3. Verify the new shared round-trip harness actually exercises both GitHub and local implementations rather than duplicating implementation-specific tests.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change — this is an internal orchestrator/runtime refactor and packaging change. ### Container (C4-L2) No new container boundary — the same Elixir application now compiles state-sync code from the new root-level folders. ### Component (C4-L3) - `state-sync/interface/lib` now owns the shared `SymphonyElixir.StateSync` behaviour and issue struct. - `state-sync-github/adapter/lib` owns GitHub issue sync and normalization. - `state-sync-github/merge-automation/lib` owns GitHub PR feedback, approval, and merge readiness helpers. - `state-sync-local/adapter/lib` owns local file config, workflow parsing, and state transitions. - `state-sync/test/lib` owns the shared round-trip contract harness used by implementation tests. ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `SymphonyElixir.StateSync` selects implementations via `Config.settings!().state.type` and exposes the shared contract. - `SymphonyElixir.Config.Schema` normalizes legacy `tracker`/`kind` input into canonical `state`/`type` config. - `SymphonyElixir.StateSync.GitHub.MergeAutomation.*` holds GitHub-only merge helpers that used to sit on the shared boundary. - `SymphonyElixir.StateSync.Test.RoundTrip` provides the reusable implementation contract checks. ### Decision Record - **Decision**: compile the shared interface and implementations from new root-level state-sync folders while keeping module names and orchestrator behavior aligned through a config compatibility layer. - **Alternatives considered**: keep the old `elixir/lib` layout with alias wrappers only; or move code without supporting legacy `tracker`/`kind` inputs during the transition. - **Trade-offs**: the repo layout becomes more explicit and portable, but the migration touches many files and requires compatibility shims/tests to keep old config paths readable. - **Why chosen**: it satisfies the ticket’s self-contained skill-folder requirement and preserves runtime behavior while the codebase migrates to the new naming. - **Implementation links**: `state-sync/interface/lib/symphony_elixir/state_sync.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/config/schema.ex`, `state-sync-github/merge-automation/lib/symphony_elixir/state_sync/github/merge_automation/auto_merge.ex`, `state-sync-local/adapter/lib/symphony_elixir/state_sync/local/project_config.ex`, `state-sync/test/lib/symphony_elixir/state_sync/test/round_trip.ex`
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
fix(bus): fast-path degraded redundant realtime bounces

Summary:
- let redundant realtime exchanges complete immediately on a single
  processed bounce instead of waiting for timeout-driven best effort
- add unit coverage for the realtime one-bounce fast path
- extend the secondary-disconnect simulator regression to a 1ms domain

Rationale:
- after the earlier timeout-budget fixes, degraded redundant operation
  was still too conservative for 1ms cyclic traffic
- cyclic PDO exchanges should favor low-latency processed data, while
  reliable/startup traffic can keep the stricter merge behavior
- this keeps the fix local to the bus link and avoids adding NIC-state
  probing just to distinguish backup-port disconnect from other cases

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/45_redundant_secondary_disconnect_cycle_budget_test.exs

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill degraded redundant hardening hash

Summary:
- replace the pending unreleased changelog hashes with the new runtime
  hardening commit id

Rationale:
- keep the unreleased notes traceable to the feature commit that added
  the degraded redundant timing and receive-error fixes

Validation:
- not run (changelog-only follow-up)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(bus): harden degraded redundant raw handling

Summary:
- surface raw receive errors through the bus transport boundary and
  degrade single/redundant links explicitly instead of silently
  ignoring receive-side failures
- keep degraded redundant exchanges within the original frame timeout
  budget and pass that bus timeout budget down to cyclic domains
- add missing secondary-break simulator coverage, redundant raw helper
  toggles, and a 10ms secondary-disconnect regression scenario

Rationale:
- a secondary-port disconnect on real hardware could keep a 10ms
  redundant ring timing out even though the primary path was still
  usable
- the root cause was split across layers: redundant links extended the
  merge wait past the exchange budget, and domains could time out
  their Bus.transaction call before the bus budget had expired
- explicit receive-error handling and transport-aware simulator
  coverage make the degraded path easier to reason about and harder to
  regress again

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs test/ethercat/simulator_redundancy_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/domain_test.exs test/ethercat/master/startup_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/45_redundant_secondary_disconnect_cycle_budget_test.exs
- mix test --failed

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill runtime hardening hash

Summary:
- replace the three unreleased pending-hash placeholders with cf4a90c

Rationale:
- keep the changelog consistent with the committed runtime change set

Validation:
- not run (changelog-only follow-up)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(runtime): harden redundant links and recovery timing

Summary:
- delay master recovery until a domain crosses a short unhealthy streak,
  and keep a wider 5ms startup/activation frame-timeout floor
- make redundant links raw-only, require authoritative forward-path data,
  and drain both legs before dispatching the next exchange
- enable PACKET_IGNORE_OUTGOING on raw sockets and align simulator
  scenarios/docs with passthrough semantics and raw transport controls

Rationale:
- avoid simulator and raw transport flapping on isolated cycle misses and
  transient SAFEOP/startup timeouts
- keep healthy-ring redundant semantics explicit while cutting stale
  previous-exchange traffic that was inflating idx_mismatch drops

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix docs --warnings-as-errors --formatter html
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/bus/link/redundant_merge_test.exs test/ethercat/simulator_redundancy_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/domain/health_notifications_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs test/ethercat/master/startup_test.exs test/ethercat/master_recovery_bus_test.exs test/ethercat/master_activation_test.exs test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/domain_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/38_redundant_passthrough_only_partial_test.exs test/integration/simulator/41_redundant_multi_datagram_bwr_test.exs

direct merge/revert on `main`.

troubleshooting, and solution index/snippet pages.
- Supersedes the accidentally merged-and-reverted path from #4946 with a
reviewable PR flow.

- [x] Confirm `git diff origin/main...HEAD` contains only the intended 4
docs files.
- [x] Verify branch is pushed and opens cleanly against `main`.
- [ ] Run CI checks in GitHub and confirm all required checks pass.
- [ ] Validate docs preview links for the changed pages.

## Context
- Previous PR: #4946

Migrate the remaining review, issue, kanban workflow, and tool specialists to YAML runtime definitions and move their English Markdown prompt copies under locales/en overlays. This leaves only the team specialists on the legacy top-level Markdown path.

commit.message:
refactor(frontend): align homepage and workspace overview flow (#203)

Unify the entry model around homepage launcher, workspace overview, and Kanban as the canonical operating surface.

- remove homepage Kanban summary duplication
- convert /workspace/[workspaceId] into a real overview surface
- align desktop navigation semantics to Overview / Kanban / Traces
- refresh affected page snapshot baselines and registry metadata
